# Haneul Documentation

> Haneul is a next-generation smart contract platform with high throughput, low latency, and an asset-oriented programming model powered by Move

## Concepts

- [Coin Management](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/coin-mgt.md)
    Description: Because Haneul uses coins as owned objects for transactions, you need to explicitly
        manage them in your programmable transaction block development.
- [Cryptography](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/cryptography.md)
    Description: Haneul supports multiple cryptography algorithms and primitives, while also defining
        its own such as public keys, signatures, aggregated signatures, and hash functions.
- [Passkey](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/cryptography/passkeys.md)
    Description: Haneul supports the passkey signature scheme that enables you to sign in to apps and
        sign transactions using a private key stored securely on a passkey authenticator. It uses
        the WebAuthn standard.
- [Checkpoint
    Verification](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/cryptography/system/checkpoint-verification.md)
    Description: On the Haneul network, checkpoints define the history of the blockchain. Checkpoint
        verification is how full nodes and other clients guarantee their state is exactly the same
        as the Haneul network.
- [Intents for
    Validation](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/cryptography/system/intents-for-validation.md)
    Description: Content coming soon
- [Validator Signatures](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/cryptography/system/validator-signatures.md)
    Description: Content coming soon
- [Archival Store and Service](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/data-access/archival-store.md)
    Description: The historical data API for fast lookups, served from a scalable and performant
        historical data store.
- [Custom Indexers](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/data-access/custom-indexers.md)
    Description: The `haneul-indexer-alt-framework` is a powerful Rust framework for building
        high-performance, custom blockchain indexers on Haneul. It provides customizable,
        production-ready components for data ingestion, processing, and storage.
- [Accessing Data](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/data-access/data-serving.md)
    Description: Overview of the types of data access mechanisms available in Haneul.
- [GraphQL for Haneul RPC (Beta)](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/data-access/graphql-rpc.md)
    Description: Use GraphQL to make Haneul RPC calls. This feature is currently in Beta.
- [gRPC](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/data-access/grpc.md)
    Description: Learn about gRPC and how it provides fast, type-safe access to Haneul network data.
- [Indexer Data and
    Integration](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/data-access/indexer-data-integration.md)
    Description: Learn how to integrate custom data sources and storage systems with Haneul indexers.
        Covers checkpoint data sources, custom store implementations, and Move event
        deserialization for building flexible indexing solutions.
- [Indexer Runtime and
    Performance](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/data-access/indexer-runtime-perf.md)
    Description: Learn how to optimize Haneul custom indexer performance through runtime
        configuration, resource monitoring, and debugging tools. Covers ingestion settings,
        database tuning, Tokio console debugging, Prometheus metrics, and data pruning strategies.
- [Indexer Pipeline Architecture](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/data-access/pipeline-architecture.md)
    Description: The `haneul-indexer-alt-framework` provides two distinct pipeline architectures.
        Understand the differences between the sequential and concurrent pipelines that the
        `haneul-indexer-alt-framework` provides to decide which best haneults your project needs.
- [Gaming on Haneul](https://docs.haneul.io/concepts/gaming.md)
    Description: Haneul offers features like dynamic NFTs, Kiosk, soulbound assets, and on-chain
        randomness to provide builders with the tools to create immersive, transparent, and fair
        gaming experiences.

## General

- [Guides](https://docs.haneul.io/guides.md)
    Description: Start developing on Haneul through example projects and developer guides.

## Guides

- [Using the Archival
    Service](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/accessing-data/archival-store.md)
    Description: Query historical Haneul network data through the Archival Service gRPC API.
- [Authenticated
    Events](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/accessing-data/authenticated-events.md)
    Description: Cryptographically verifiable Move events that can be verified by a light client
        without trusting any intermediary, suitable for trustless event consumption.
- [Bring Your Own Store
    (BYOS)](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/accessing-data/custom-indexer/bring-your-own-store.md)
    Description: Implement a custom storage backend for the customer indexer framework.
- [Build a Custom
    Indexer](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/accessing-data/custom-indexer/build.md)
    Description: Build a custom indexer using the `haneul-indexer-alt-framework` module. The example
        indexer demonstrates a sequential pipeline that extracts transaction digests from Haneul
        checkpoints and stores them in a local PostgreSQL.
- [Custom Indexing Framework](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/accessing-data/custom-indexer.md)
    Description: The `haneul-indexer-alt-framework` is a powerful Rust framework for building
        high-performance, custom blockchain indexers on Haneul. It provides customizable,
        production-ready components for data ingestion, processing, and storage.
- [Custom Indexer and
    Walrus](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/accessing-data/custom-indexer/indexer-walrus.md)
    Description: Walrus is a content-addressable storage protocol, where data is retrieved using a
        unique identifier derived from the content itself, rather than from a file path or
        location. Integrating a custom Haneul Indexer with Walrus can provide novel user experiences.
- [Using gRPC](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/accessing-data/grpc-overview.md)
    Description: Learn how to use gRPC clients for the Haneul network with grpcurl, Buf CLI, and
        popular programming languages.
- [Using GraphQL RPC](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/accessing-data/query-with-graphql.md)
    Description: Practical guide to making queries of the Haneul RPC using the GraphQL service, with
        examples for common tasks.
- [Using Events](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/accessing-data/using-events.md)
    Description: Use events to notify on-chain assets of activity your smart contracts initiate and
        query events from other packages to trigger logic based on emitted events.
- [Address Balances Migration
    Guide](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/address-balances-migration.md)
    Description: Migrating from Coin objects to address balances on Haneul.
- [Auction](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/auction.md)
    Description: Content coming soon
- [Blackjack](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/blackjack.md)
    Description: Learn Haneul using an example implementation of the popular casino game Blackjack.
- [Client App with Haneul TypeScript
    SDK](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/client-tssdk.md)
    Description: Build a client-side application using the Haneul TypeScript SDK.
- [Coin Flip](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/coin-flip.md)
    Description: Learn Haneul through a coin flip app that covers the full end-to-end flow of building
        a Haneul Move module and connecting it to a React Haneul app.
- [Distributed Counter](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/e2e-counter.md)
    Description: An app that allows users to create counters that anyone can increment, but only
        the owner can reset.
- [App Examples](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples.md)
    Description: Practical examples written by Haneul and Move experts. Use these to learn common
        patterns and apply techniques to your own projects.
- [Meta and Pricing
    Oracle](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/meta-pricing-oracle.md)
    Description: Content coming soon
- [Oracles](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/oracle.md)
    Description: Oracles connect smart contracts deployed on-chain with data that is stored
        off-chain.
- [Plinko](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/plinko.md)
    Description: Learn Haneul through an example implementation of the popular casino game, Plinko.
- [Prevent Bots](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/recaptcha.md)
    Description: Write and deploy a smart contract in Move that uses reCAPTCHA to verify users are
        human (and not bots) before they interact with the contract.
- [Review Rating](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/reviews-rating.md)
    Description: This example app creates a food rating service that stores all review data and
        algorithms on-chain.
- [Tic-Tac-Toe](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/tic-tac-toe.md)
    Description: This example demonstrates how to create three variations of a tic-tac-toe app on
        Haneul.
- [Trustless Swap](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/trustless-swap.md)
    Description: An app that performs atomic swaps on Haneul. Atomic swaps are similar to escrows but
        without requiring a trusted third party.
- [Turnip Town](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/turnip-town.md)
    Description: Content coming soon
- [Haneul Weather Oracle](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/app-examples/weather-oracle.md)
    Description: Write a module (smart contract) in Move that fetches the weather data from the
        OpenWeather API every 10 minutes and updates the weather conditions for over 1,000
        locations around the world.
- [Create Currencies and Tokens](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/coin/currency.md)
    Description: Learn how to create currencies and mint coins and tokens on the Haneul network using
        the Coin Registry system.
- [In-Game Currency](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/coin/in-game-token.md)
    Description: Use the Haneul Closed-Loop Token standard to create tokens that you can use as
        currency within a game application.
- [Currencies and Tokens](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/coin.md)
    Description: Learn about creating and managing currencies and tokens on Haneul using the Coin
        Registry system and `token` module, including regulated tokens, in-game currencies, and
        loyalty tokens.
- [Loyalty Tokens](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/coin/loyalty.md)
    Description: Use the Haneul Closed-Loop Token standard to create tokens that are only valid within
        specific workflows and services. One example of Closed-Loop Tokens is a loyalty token.
- [Regulated Currency and Deny List](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/coin/regulated.md)
    Description: You can create regulated currencies on Haneul using the Coin Registry system. These
        coins include the ability to control access using a deny list.
- [Stablecoins on Haneul](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/coin/stablecoins.md)
    Description: Stablecoins are a type of cryptocurrency that are designed to maintain a stable
        value relative to a fiat currency or a basket of assets.
- [Token Vesting Strategies](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/coin/vesting-strategies.md)
    Description: If you plan to launch a token on Haneul, then you might consider implementing a
        vesting strategy to strengthen the long-term outlook of your token.
- [Troubleshooting Common Errors](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/common-errors.md)
    Description: Find solutions to common issues with addresses, transactions, and Move packages.
- [Elliptic Curve Verifiable Random
    Function](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/cryptography/ecvrf.md)
    Description: Elliptic curve verifiable random function is a cryptographic algorithm that
        enables you to generate a random number and provide proof that the number used a secret key
        for generation.
- [Groth16](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/cryptography/groth16.md)
    Description: Zero-knowledge proofs are used to validate statements without revealing
        information about the proof's inputs.
- [Hashing](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/cryptography/hashing.md)
    Description: Haneul supports SHA2-256, SHA3-256, Keccak256, and Blake2b-256 cryptographic hash
        functions.
- [Cryptography](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/cryptography.md)
    Description: Learn how to use cryptography effectively in Haneul smart contracts.
- [Multisig Authentication](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/cryptography/multisig.md)
    Description: Guide on how to create a multisig transaction and then submit it against a local
        network using the Haneul CLI.
- [Haneul On-Chain Signatures Verification in
    Move](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/cryptography/signing.md)
    Description: Haneul supports verification within Move smart contracts through several signature
        schemes. Signature schemes include Ed25519, Secp256k1 recoverable, Secp256k1
        non-recoverable, Secp256r1 non-recoverable, Secp256r1 recoverable, BLS G1, and BLS G2.
- [Configure OpenID
    Providers](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/cryptography/zklogin-integration/developer-account.md)
    Description: zkLogin can be integrated with an application using an OpenID provider's OAuth
        Client ID and redirect URI.
- [zkLogin](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/cryptography/zklogin-integration.md)
    Description: zkLogin is a Haneul primitive that enables you to send transactions from a Haneul
        address using an OAuth credential without publicly linking the two.
- [zkLogin
    Example](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/cryptography/zklogin-integration/zklogin-example.md)
    Description: An example that breaks down the logic behind each step of zkLogin.
- [zkLogin
    Integration](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/cryptography/zklogin-integration/zklogin-integration.md)
    Description: zkLogin can be integrated into applications deployed on Haneul.
- [What is
    zkLogin?](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/cryptography/zklogin-integration/zklogin.md)
    Description: zkLogin is a Haneul primitive that enables you to send transactions from a Haneul
        address using an OAuth credential without publicly linking the two.
- [Haneul Developer Cheat Sheet](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/dev-cheat-sheet.md)
    Description: A quick reference document regarding best practices for Haneul developers.
- [Connect a Frontend to a Move
    Package](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started/app-frontends.md)
    Description: Connect a React interface to a basic Move package to create a full stack
        application.
- [Configure a Haneul
    Client](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started/configure-haneul-client.md)
    Description: The Haneul client configuration specifies which network to connect to and which
        address to send transactions.
- [Create a Haneul Address](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started/get-address.md)
    Description: You need an address on the Haneul network before you can build packages and own
        objects.
- [Get HANEUL from Faucet](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started/get-coins.md)
    Description: Use the Haneul faucet to obtain free HANEUL tokens for use on the Haneul Devnet and Testnet
        networks.
- [Hello, World!](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started/hello-world.md)
    Description: Create and publish your first Move package using a basic 'Hello, World!' example.
- [Getting Started](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started.md)
    Description: Get started with Haneul, the first internet-scale programmable blockchain platform.
- [Install from Binaries](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started/install-binaries.md)
    Description: Each Haneul release provides a set of binaries for several operating systems. You can
        download these binaries from GitHub and use them to install Haneul.
- [Install from Source](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started/install-source.md)
    Description: Install the Haneul framework from source, either locally or directly from GitHub.
- [Connecting to a Local
    Network](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started/local-network.md)
    Description: Connect to a Haneul local network using the Haneul CLI. Use local networks to test your
        applications and prepare for launching them on Devnet or Testnet.
- [Next Steps](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started/next-steps.md)
    Description: To continue your journey building on Haneul, you can review other documentation, join
        the community of other Haneul builders, or check out the Awesome Haneul repo.
- [Install Haneul](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started/haneul-install.md)
    Description: Install the Haneul framework and its required prerequisites on your system.
- [HANEUL Wallets](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/getting-started/haneul-wallets.md)
    Description: placeholder
- [Move Best Practices](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/move-best-practices.md)
    Description: Recommended Move 2024 best practices for Haneul development.
- [Customizing, Testing, and
    Management](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/nautilus/customize-nautilus.md)
    Description: Details on how to customize Nautilus server logic, test functionality locally, and
        manage the enclave.
- [Nautilus](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/nautilus.md)
    Description: Run secure, off-chain logic in trusted execution environments (TEEs), and verify
        it on-chain to trigger safe smart contract workflows.
- [Deploy Dockerized Nautilus Apps to
    Marlin](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/nautilus/marlin.md)
    Description: Marlin Oyster integrates with the Nautilus framework to remove the operational
        overhead of managing enclave infrastructure.
- [Nautilus Design](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/nautilus/nautilus-design.md)
    Description: Overview of the design aspects of Nautilus, including its trust model.
- [What is Nautilus?](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/nautilus/nautilus-overview.md)
    Description: Nautilus is a verifiable off-chain compute layer on Haneul.
- [Encrypt Enclave Secrets with Seal](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/nautilus/seal.md)
    Description: The Seal-Nautilus pattern provides secure secret management for enclave
        applications.
- [Using Nautilus](https://docs.haneul.io/guides/developer/nautilus/using-nautilus.md)
    Description: Details on how to use Nautilus with the reproducible build template.

## References

- [Awesome Haneul Gaming](https://docs.haneul.io/references/awesome-haneul-gaming.md)
    Description: A curated list of awesome gaming projects and developer tools within the Haneul
        ecosystem.
- [Awesome Haneul](https://docs.haneul.io/references/awesome-haneul.md)
    Description: A curated list of awesome developer tools and infrastructure projects within the
        Haneul ecosystem.
- [Haneul CLI](https://docs.haneul.io/references/cli.md)
    Description: Haneul provides command line tools to interact with the network, its features, and
        the Move programming language. Individual command groups are referred to as Haneul Client CLI,
        Haneul Keytool CLI, Haneul Move CLI, and Haneul Validator CLI.
- [Haneul CLI Cheat Sheet](https://docs.haneul.io/references/cli/cheatsheet.md)
    Description: Use this cheat sheet to learn common Haneul CLI commands.
- [Haneul Client CLI](https://docs.haneul.io/references/cli/client.md)
    Description: The Haneul Client CLI provides command-level access to interact with the Haneul network.
- [Haneul Keytool CLI](https://docs.haneul.io/references/cli/keytool.md)
    Description: The Haneul Keytool CLI has commands for managing and generating addresses, working
        with private keys, signatures, or zkLogin.
- [Haneul Move CLI](https://docs.haneul.io/references/cli/move.md)
    Description: The Haneul CLI move command provides commands for working with Move source code
        directly from a terminal or console.
- [Haneul Client PTB CLI](https://docs.haneul.io/references/cli/ptb.md)
    Description: The Haneul Client PTB CLI enables a user to construct a PTB and execute it from the
        command line or a file.
- [Haneul Replay CLI](https://docs.haneul.io/references/cli/replay.md)
    Description: Use the Haneul CLI to replay a given transaction to view its effects.
- [Haneul Trace Analysis](https://docs.haneul.io/references/cli/trace-analysis.md)
    Description: Use the Haneul CLI to analyze transaction traces.
- [Haneul Validator CLI](https://docs.haneul.io/references/cli/validator.md)
    Description: The Haneul Validator CLI is used to access the validator features of the Haneul network.
- [Haneul Contributor Covenant Code of
    Conduct](https://docs.haneul.io/references/contribute/code-of-conduct.md)
    Description: All contributions to Haneul must adhere to the Contributor Code of Conduct.
- [Contribute to Haneul
    Repositories](https://docs.haneul.io/references/contribute/contribute-to-haneul-repos.md)
    Description: Haneul repositories are open source and welcome community contributions.
- [Contribute to Haneul
    Documentation](https://docs.haneul.io/references/contribute/contribution-process.md)
    Description: Help the Haneul community through documentation contributions. Whether its to fix
        errors or add new content, the entire Haneul community benefits from your contributions.
- [Localize Haneul Documentation](https://docs.haneul.io/references/contribute/localize-haneul-docs.md)
    Description: The Haneul documentation can be translated into any language using Crowdin.
- [MDX Components](https://docs.haneul.io/references/contribute/mdx-components.md)
    Description: Extensions to the Haneul docs rendering engine.
- [Style Guide](https://docs.haneul.io/references/contribute/style-guide.md)
    Description: All contributions to the Haneul documentation must adhere to the style guide.
- [Haneul Environment Setup](https://docs.haneul.io/references/contribute/haneul-environment.md)
    Description: Get the background information you need before you start developing on Haneul. Learn
        the layout of the Haneul monorepository and the suggested development environment for working
        with Move.
- [Exchange Integration Guide](https://docs.haneul.io/references/exchange-integration-guide.md)
    Description: Learn the primary tasks necessary to integrate the HANEUL token with a cryptocurrency
        exchange.
- [Haneul Framework](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework.md)
    Description: The Haneul framework libraries include Move modules that provide the logic for Haneul
        and its standards. A Rust process creates the documentation for the modules directly from
        comments in the code.
- [Module bridge::bridge](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_bridge/bridge.md)
    Description: Struct Bridge Struct BridgeInner Struct TokenDepositedEvent Struct
        TokenDepositedEventV2 Struct EmergencyOpEvent Struct BridgeRecord Struct
        TokenTransferApproved Struct TokenTransferClaimed Struct TokenTransferAlreadyApproved
        Struct TokenTransferAlreadyClaimed Struct TokenTransferLimitExceed
- [Module bridge::chain_ids](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_bridge/chain_ids.md)
    Description: Struct BridgeRoute Constants Function haneulmainnet Function suitestnet Function
        haneulcustom Function ethmainnet Function ethsepolia Function ethcustom Function routesource
        Function routedestination Function assertvalidchainid Function validroutes Function
        isvalidroute Function getroute use ; Struct
- [Module bridge::committee](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_bridge/committee.md)
    Description: Struct BlocklistValidatorEvent Struct BridgeCommittee Struct CommitteeUpdateEvent
        Struct CommitteeMemberUrlUpdateEvent Struct CommitteeMember Struct
        CommitteeMemberRegistration Constants Function verifysignatures Function create Function
        register Function trycreatenextcommittee Function
- [Module bridge::crypto](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_bridge/crypto.md)
    Description: Function ecdsapubkeytoethaddress use ; use ; Function public(package) fun
        (compressedpubkey: &vector): vector
- [haneul:bridge](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_bridge.md)
    Description: Documentation for the modules in the haneul/crates/haneul-framework/packages/bridge
        crate. Select a module from the list to see its details.
- [Module bridge::limiter](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_bridge/limiter.md)
    Description: Struct TransferLimiter Struct TransferRecord Struct UpdateRouteLimitEvent
        Constants Function getroutelimit Function new Function checkandrecordsendingtransfer
        Function updateroutelimit Function currenthoursinceepoch Function adjusttransferrecords
        Function initialtransferlimits use ; use ; use ; use
- [Module bridge::message](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_bridge/message.md)
    Description: Struct BridgeMessage Struct BridgeMessageKey Struct TokenTransferPayload Struct
        TokenTransferPayloadV2 Struct EmergencyOp Struct Blocklist Struct UpdateBridgeLimit Struct
        UpdateAssetPrice Struct AddTokenOnHaneul Struct ParsedTokenTransferMessage Constants Function
        extracttokenbridgepayload Function
- [Module
    bridge::message_types](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_bridge/message_types.md)
    Description: Constants Function token Function committeeblocklist Function emergencyop Function
        updatebridgelimit Function updateassetprice Function addtokensonhaneul Constants const : u8 =
        0; const : u8 = 1; const : u8 = 2; const : u8 = 3; const : u8 = 4; const : u8 = 6; Function
        public fun (): u8 Function public
- [Module bridge::treasury](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_bridge/treasury.md)
    Description: Struct BridgeTreasury Struct BridgeTokenMetadata Struct ForeignTokenRegistration
        Struct UpdateTokenPriceEvent Struct NewTokenEvent Struct TokenRegistrationEvent Constants
        Function tokenid Function decimalmultiplier Function notionalvalue Function
        registerforeigntoken Function addnewtoken Function
- [Module std::address](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/address.md)
    Description: Provides a way to get address length since it's a platform-specific parameter.
        Function length Function Should be converted to a native function. Current implementation
        only works for Haneul.
- [Module std::ascii](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/ascii.md)
    Description: The ASCII module defines basic string and char newtypes in Move that verify that
        characters are valid ASCII, and that strings consist of only valid ASCII characters.
- [Module std::bcs](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/bcs.md)
    Description: Utility for converting a Move value to its binary representation in BCS (Binary
        Canonical. Serialization). BCS is the binary encoding for Move resources and other
        non-module values published on-chain. See
        https://github.com/diem/bcs#binary-canonical-serialization-bcs for more details on BCS.
- [Module std::bit_vector](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/bit_vector.md)
    Description: Struct BitVector Constants Function new Function set Function unset Function
        shiftleft Function isindexset Function length Function longestsetsequencestartingat Struct
        public struct has copy, drop, store Fields Constants The provided index is out of bounds
        const : = 131072; An invalid length of
- [Module std::debug](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/debug.md)
    Description: Module providing debug functionality.
- [Module std::fixed_point32](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/fixed_point32.md)
    Description: Defines a fixed-point numeric type with a 32-bit integer part and a 32-bit
        fractional part.
- [Module std::hash](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/hash.md)
    Description: Module which defines SHA hashes for byte vectors. The functions in this module are
        natively declared both in the Move runtime as in the Move prover's prelude.
- [haneul:std](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std.md)
    Description: Documentation for the modules in the haneul/crates/haneul-framework/packages/std crate.
        Select a module from the list to see its details.
- [Module std::internal](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/internal.md)
    Description: Defines the type, which can be used to constrain the logic of a generic function
        to be authorized only by the module that defines the type parameter. To write a function
        that is guarded by a , require it as an argument. Struct Permit Function permit Struct A
        privileged witness of the T type.
- [Module std::macros](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/macros.md)
    Description: This module holds shared implementation of macros used in std Macro function
        nummax Macro function nummin Macro function numdiff Macro function numdivideandroundup
        Macro function numpow Macro function numsqrt Macro function numtostring Macro function
        numcheckedadd Macro function numcheckedsub Macro
- [Module std::option](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/option.md)
    Description: This module defines the Option type and its methods to represent and handle an
        optional value.
- [Module std::string](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/string.md)
    Description: The module defines the type which represents UTF8 encoded strings.
- [Module std::type_name](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/type_name.md)
    Description: Functionality for converting Move types into values. Use with care!
- [Module std::u128](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/u128.md)
    Description: Function bitwisenot Function max Function min Function diff Function
        divideandroundup Function pow Function sqrt Function tryasu8 Function tryasu16 Function
        tryasu32 Function tryasu64 Function tostring Function checkedadd Function checkedsub
        Function checkedmul Function checkeddiv Function

        saturatingsub Function saturatingmul Function checkedshl
- [Module std::uq32_32](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/uq32_32.md)
    Description: Defines an unsigned, fixed-point numeric type with a 32-bit integer part and a
        32-bit fractional part. The notation and is based on Q notation). q indicates it a
        fixed-point number. The u prefix indicates it is unsigned.
- [Module std::uq64_64](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/uq64_64.md)
    Description: Defines an unsigned, fixed-point numeric type with a 64-bit integer part and a
        64-bit fractional part. The notation and is based on Q notation). q indicates it a
        fixed-point number. The u prefix indicates it is unsigned.
- [Module std::vector](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_std/vector.md)
    Description: A variable-sized container that can hold any type. Indexing is 0-based, and
        vectors are growable. This module has many native functions.
- [Module haneul::accumulator](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/accumulator.md)

- [Module
    haneul::authenticator_state](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/authenticator_state.md)
    Description: Struct AuthenticatorState Struct AuthenticatorStateInner Struct JWK Struct JwkId
        Struct ActiveJwk Constants Function activejwkequal Function jwkequal Function jwkidequal
        Function stringbyteslt Function jwklt Function create Function loadinnermut Function
        loadinner Function checksorted Function
- [Module haneul::bag](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/bag.md)
    Description: A bag is a heterogeneous map-like collection. The collection is similar to in that
        its keys and values are not stored within the value, but instead are stored using Haneul's
        object system. The struct acts only as a handle into the object system to retrieve those
        keys and values.
- [Module haneul::balance](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/balance.md)
    Description: A storable handler for Balances in general. Is used in the Coin module to allow
        balance operations and can be used to implement custom coins with and s.
- [Module haneul::bcs](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/bcs.md)
    Description: This module implements BCS (de)serialization in Move.
- [Module haneul::bls12381](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/bls12381.md)
    Description: Group operations of BLS12-381.
- [Module haneul::borrow](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/borrow.md)
    Description: A simple library that enables hot-potato-locked borrow mechanics. With
        Programmable transactions, it is possible to borrow a value within a transaction, use it
        and put back in the end. Hot-potato makes sure the object is returned and was not swapped
        for another one.
- [Module haneul::clock](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/clock.md)
    Description: APIs for accessing time from move calls, via the : a unique shared object that is
        created at 0x6 during genesis.
- [Module haneul::coin](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/coin.md)
    Description: Defines the type - platform wide representation of fungible tokens and coins. can
        be described as a secure wrapper around Balance type.
- [Module haneul::coin_registry](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/coin_registry.md)
    Description: Defines the system object for managing coin data in a central registry. This
        module provides a centralized way to store and manage metadata for all currencies in the
        Haneul ecosystem, including their supply information, regulatory status, and metadata
        capabilities.
- [Module haneul::config](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/config.md)
    Description: Struct Config Struct Setting Struct SettingData Constants Function new Function
        share Function transfer Function addfornextepoch Function removefornextepoch Function
        existswithtype Function existswithtypefornextepoch Function borrowfornextepochmut Function
        readsettingfornextepoch Macro function
- [Module haneul::deny_list](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/deny_list.md)
    Description: Defines the type. The shared object is used to restrict access to instances of
        certain core types from being used as inputs by specified addresses in the deny list.
- [Module haneul::derived_object](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/derived_object.md)
    Description: Enables the creation of objects with deterministic addresses derived from a parent
        object's UID.
- [Module haneul::display](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/display.md)
    Description: Defines a Display struct which defines the way an Object should be displayed. The
        intention is to keep data as independent from its display as possible, protecting the
        development process and keeping it separate from the ecosystem agreements.
- [Module haneul::dynamic_field](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/dynamic_field.md)
    Description: In addition to the fields declared in its type definition, a Haneul object can have
        dynamic fields that can be added after the object has been constructed.
- [Module
    haneul::dynamic_object_field](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/dynamic_object_field.md)
    Description: Similar to , this module allows for the access of dynamic fields. But unlike, the
        values bound to these dynamic fields must be objects themselves. This allows for the
        objects to still exist within in storage, which may be important for external tools.
- [Module haneul::ecdsa_k1](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/ecdsa_k1.md)
    Description: Constants Function secp256k1ecrecover Function decompresspubkey Function
        secp256k1verify Constants Error if the public key cannot be recovered from the signature.
        const : u64 = 0; Error if the signature is invalid. const : u64 = 1; Error if the public
        key is invalid.
- [Module haneul::ecdsa_r1](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/ecdsa_r1.md)
    Description: Constants Function secp256r1ecrecover Function secp256r1verify Constants Error if
        the public key cannot be recovered from the signature. const : u64 = 0; Error if the
        signature is invalid. const : u64 = 1; Hash function name that are valid for ecrecover and
        secp256k1verify.
- [Module haneul::ecvrf](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/ecvrf.md)
    Description: Constants Function ecvrfverify Constants const : u64 = 1; const : u64 = 2; const :
        u64 = 3; Function @param hash: The hash/output from a ECVRF to be verified. @param
        alphastring: Input/seed to the ECVRF used to generate the output.
- [Module haneul::ed25519](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/ed25519.md)
    Description: Function ed25519verify Function @param signature: 32-byte signature that is a
        point on the Ed25519 elliptic curve. @param publickey: 32-byte signature that is a point on
        the Ed25519 elliptic curve. @param msg: The message that we test the signature against.
- [Module haneul::event](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/event.md)
    Description: Events module. Defines the function which creates and sends a custom MoveEvent as
        a part of the effects certificate of the transaction.
- [Module
    haneul::funds_accumulator](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/funds_accumulator.md)
    Description: A module for accumulating funds, i.e. Balance-like types.
- [Module haneul::groth16](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/groth16.md)
    Description: Struct Curve Struct PreparedVerifyingKey Struct PublicProofInputs Struct
        ProofPoints Constants Function bls12381 Function bn254 Function pvkfrombytes Function
        pvktobytes Function publicproofinputsfrombytes Function proofpointsfrombytes Function
        prepareverifyingkey Function
- [Module haneul::group_ops](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/group_ops.md)
    Description: Generic Move and native functions for group operations.
- [Module haneul::hash](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/hash.md)
    Description: Module which defines hash functions. Note that Sha-256 and Sha3-256 is available
        in the std::hash module in the standard library. Function blake2b256 Function keccak256
        Function @param data: Arbitrary binary data to hash. Hash the input bytes using Blake2b-256
        and returns 32 bytes.
- [Module haneul::hex](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/hex.md)
    Description: HEX (Base16) encoding utility.
- [Module haneul::hmac](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/hmac.md)
    Description: Function hmacsha3256 Function @param key: HMAC key, arbitrary bytes. @param msg:
        message to sign, arbitrary bytes. Returns the 32 bytes digest of HMAC-SHA3-256(key, msg).
- [haneul:haneul](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul.md)
    Description: Documentation for the modules in the haneul/crates/haneul-framework/packages/haneul crate.
        Select a module from the list to see its details.
- [Module haneul::kiosk](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/kiosk.md)
    Description: Kiosk is a primitive for building safe, decentralized and trustless trading
        experiences. It allows storing and trading any types of assets as long as the creator of
        these assets implements a TransferPolicy for them.
- [Module haneul::kiosk_extension](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/kiosk_extension.md)
    Description: This module implements the Kiosk Extensions functionality. It allows exposing
        previously protected (only-owner) methods to third-party apps. A Kiosk Extension is a
        module that implements any functionality on top of the Kiosk without discarding nor
        blocking the base.
- [Module haneul::linked_table](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/linked_table.md)
    Description: Similar to but the values are linked together, allowing for ordered insertion and
        removal Struct LinkedTable Struct Node Constants Function new Function front Function back
        Function pushfront Function pushback Function borrow Function borrowmut Function prev
        Function next Function remove Function
- [Module haneul::math](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/math.md)
    Description: DEPRECATED, use the each integer type's individual module instead, e.g.

    haneul::nitro_attestation](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/nitro_attestation.md)
    Description: Struct PCREntry Struct NitroAttestationDocument Constants Function
        loadnitroattestation Function moduleid Function timestamp Function digest Function pcrs
        Function publickey Function userdata Function nonce Function index Function value Function

- [Module haneul::object](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/object.md)

        idtoaddress Function idfrombytes Function idfromaddress Function haneulsystemstate Function
        clock Function authenticatorstate Function randomnessstate Function haneuldenylistobjectid
        Function haneulaccumulatorrootobjectid
- [Module haneul::object_bag](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/object_bag.md)
    Description: Similar to , an is a heterogeneous map-like collection. But unlike , the values
        bound to these dynamic fields must be objects themselves. This allows for the objects to
        still exist in storage, which may be important for external tools. The difference is
        otherwise not observable from within Move.
- [Module haneul::object_table](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/object_table.md)
    Description: Similar to , an is a map-like collection. But unlike , the values bound to these
        dynamic fields must be objects themselves. This allows for the objects to still exist
        within in storage, which may be important for external tools. The difference is otherwise
        not observable from within Move.
- [Module haneul::package](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/package.md)
    Description: Functions for operating on Move packages from within Move: Creating
        proof-of-publish objects from one-time witnesses Administering package upgrades through
        upgrade policies.
- [Module haneul::party](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/party.md)
    Description: Struct Party Struct Permissions Constants Function singleowner Macro function
        transfer Macro function publictransfer Function empty Function setpermissions Function
        issingleowner Function intonative use ; use ; use ; Struct The permissions that apply to a
        party object.
- [Module haneul::pay](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/pay.md)
    Description: This module provides handy functionality for wallets and haneul::Coin management.
- [Module haneul::poseidon](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/poseidon.md)
    Description: Module which defines instances of the poseidon hash functions. Constants Function
        poseidonbn254 Function poseidonbn254internal use ; use ; use ; use ; use ; use ; use ; use
        ; Constants Error if any of the inputs are larger than or equal to the BN254 field size.
- [Module haneul::priority_queue](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/priority_queue.md)
    Description: Priority queue implemented using a max heap.
- [Module haneul::protocol_config](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/protocol_config.md)
    Description: This module provides access to protocol configuration feature flags. Feature flags
        control the availability of various protocol features and are enabled/disabled at specific
        protocol versions during epoch changes.
- [Module haneul::random](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/random.md)
    Description: This module provides functionality for generating secure randomness.
- [Module haneul::ristretto255](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/ristretto255.md)
    Description: Group operations of BLS12-381. Only available in devnet.
- [Module haneul::haneul](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/haneul.md)
    Description: Coin is the token used to pay for gas in Haneul. It has 9 decimals, and the smallest
        unit (10^-9) is called "geunhwa".
- [Module haneul::table](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/table.md)
    Description: A table is a map-like collection. But unlike a traditional collection, it's keys
        and values are not stored within the value, but instead are stored using Haneul's object
        system. The struct acts only as a handle into the object system to retrieve those keys and
        values.
- [Module haneul::table_vec](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/table_vec.md)
    Description: A basic scalable vector library implemented using Table.
- [Module haneul::token](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/token.md)
    Description: The Token module which implements a Closed Loop Token with a configurable policy.
        The policy is defined by a set of rules that must be satisfied for an action to be
        performed on the token. The module is designed to be used with a TreasuryCap to allow for
        minting and burning of the s.
- [Module haneul::transfer](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/transfer.md)
    Description: Struct Receiving Constants Function transfer Function publictransfer Function
        partytransfer Function publicpartytransfer Function freezeobject Function
        publicfreezeobject Function shareobject Function publicshareobject Function receive
        Function publicreceive Function receivingobjectid Function
- [Module haneul::transfer_policy](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/transfer_policy.md)
    Description: Defines the type and the logic to approve s. TransferPolicy - is a highly
        customizable primitive, which provides an interface for the type owner to set custom
        transfer rules for every deal performed in the Kiosk or a similar system that integrates
        with TP.
- [Module haneul::tx_context](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/tx_context.md)
    Description: Struct TxContext Function sender Function nativesender Function digest Function
        epoch Function nativeepoch Function epochtimestampms Function nativeepochtimestampms
        Function sponsor Function freshobjectaddress Function freshid Function referencegasprice
        Function nativergp Function gasprice Function
- [Module haneul::types](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/types.md)
    Description: Haneul types helpers and utilities Function isonetimewitness Function Tests if the
        argument type is a one-time witness, that is a type with only one instantiation across the
        entire code base.
- [Module haneul::url](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul/url.md)
    Description: URL: standard Uniform Resource Locator string Struct Url Function newunsafe
        Function newunsafefrombytes Function innerurl Function update use ; use ; use ; Struct
        Standard Uniform Resource Locator (URL) string.

        requestwithdrawstake Function redeemfungiblestakedhaneul Function
- [Module

- [Module
    haneul_system::haneul_system](https://docs.haneul.io/references/framework/haneul_haneul_system/haneul_system.md)
    Description: Haneul System State Type Upgrade Guide is a thin wrapper around HaneulSystemStateInner
        that provides a versioned interface. The object has a fixed ID 0x5, and the
        HaneulSystemStateInner object is stored as a dynamic field.

- [Haneul Full Node gRPC](https://docs.haneul.io/references/fullnode-protocol.md)
    Description: The Haneul full node gRPC protocol is available on all Haneul Full nodes.
- [Move Trace Debugger](https://docs.haneul.io/references/ide/debugger.md)
    Description: Debug Move execution traces directly in VS Code to understand how your code
        executes.
- [Move Analyzer VS Code Extension](https://docs.haneul.io/references/ide/move.md)
    Description: The Move extension provides language support features like code navigation,
        completion, and diagnostics for Move source code.
- [Manifest Reference](https://docs.haneul.io/references/package-managers/manifest-reference.md)
    Description: Explore examples of package manifest files.
- [Package Management
    Migration](https://docs.haneul.io/references/package-managers/package-manager-migration.md)
    Description: Learn how to migrate from the previous package management system to the new,
        optimized system.
- [PTB Commands](https://docs.haneul.io/references/ptb-commands.md)
    Description: This topic describes each PTB command and their format, return type, and signature.
- [Release Notes](https://docs.haneul.io/references/release-notes.md)
    Description: --- 🔶 Testnet | Source: GitHub Release #25364: test only changes, no user impact
        #25556: Enables the address aliases feature on mainnet. #25585: Add a new protocol version
        to support some refactoring in execution. #25261: Error field is no longer available in
        simulateResult and ExecutionResult.
- [Rust SDK](https://docs.haneul.io/references/rust-sdk.md)
    Description: The Haneul Rust SDK provides Rust wrappers around the Haneul API. Using the SDK, you can
        interact with Haneul networks using the Rust programming language.
- [Haneul RPC](https://docs.haneul.io/references/haneul-api.md)
    Description: HaneulJSON is a JSON-based format that enables alignment of JSON inputs with Move
        call arguments.

    Description: Consider RPC best practices when configuring your RPC settings.

        <>Specification⎘ export const Badge = (props) => <>{props.text} export const Details = ({
        dataOpen, dataClose, children, startOpen = false }) => { const [open, setOpen] =
        useState(startOpen); return ( { e.preventDefault();
-
    [RegulatedState](https://docs.haneul.io/references/haneul-api/haneul-graphql/beta/reference/types/enums/regulated-state.md)

        dataOpen, dataClose, children, startOpen = false }) => { const [open, setOpen] =

## Standards

- [Closed-Loop Token](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/closed-loop-token.md)
    Description: Closed-Loop tokens can only be used for a specific service or by authorized users.
- [Action Request](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/closed-loop-token/action-request.md)
    Description: Tokens do not allow transfers, conversions, or spends by default. To allow these
        actions, use an `ActionRequest`.
- [Coin/Token API
    comparison](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/closed-loop-token/coin-token-comparison.md)
    Description: Compare and contrast the API used for the `coin` and `token` modules.
- [Rules](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/closed-loop-token/rules.md)
    Description: Rule modules are used to create programmable restrictions that apply any action
        within a `TokenPolicy`.
- [Spending](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/closed-loop-token/spending.md)
    Description: Token types cannot use the `store` ability to store other objects. Instead, Tokens
        have a `spend` method.
- [Token Policy](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/closed-loop-token/token-policy.md)
    Description: A `TokenPolicy` is a shared object that defines a token's allowed actions and
        conditions. Wallets and other financial services often use `TokenPolicy` objects.
- [Coin Standard](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/coin.md)
    Description: The Coin standard enables you to create a broad range of fungible tokens on the
        Haneul network to satisfy a number of use cases.
- [Currency Standard](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/currency.md)
    Description: The Haneul Currency Standard enables you to create a broad range of fungible tokens
        on the Haneul network using either the legacy coin creation or the centralized coin registry
        system.
- [DeepBook Margin Indexer](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin-indexer.md)
    Description: DeepBook Margin Indexer provides access to margin trading events including loans,
        liquidations, and margin pool operations.
- [DeepBook Margin SDK](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin-sdk.md)
    Description: Use the DeepBook TypeScript SDK to interact directly with the DeepBook Margin
        package.
- [Maintainer SDK](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin-sdk/maintainer.md)
    Description: Learn how to use the DeepBook Margin SDK to manage margin pools and system
        configuration.
- [Margin Manager SDK](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin-sdk/margin-manager.md)
    Description: Learn how to use the DeepBook Margin SDK to manage margin accounts and leveraged
        positions.
- [Margin Pool SDK](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin-sdk/margin-pool.md)
    Description: Learn how to use the DeepBook Margin SDK to supply liquidity and manage positions
        in margin pools.
- [Orders SDK](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin-sdk/orders.md)
    Description: Learn how to use the DeepBook Margin SDK to place and manage orders with margin
        managers.
- [Take Profit Stop Loss SDK](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin-sdk/tpsl.md)
    Description: Learn how to use the DeepBook Margin SDK to manage conditional orders for
        automated trading.
- [DeepBook Margin](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin.md)
    Description: Leveraged trading capabilities for DeepBookV3, enabling users to trade with
        borrowed funds and increase their buying power.
- [Contract Information](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin/contract-information.md)
    Description: DeepBook Margin contract addresses, supported coins, margin pools, and risk
        parameters on Haneul Mainnet.
- [Interest
    Rates](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin/contract-information/interest-rates.md)
    Description: Learn how interest rates are calculated for margin pool borrowing.
- [Maintainer](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin/contract-information/maintainer.md)
    Description: Learn about maintainer functions for managing margin pools and system
        configuration.
- [Margin
    Manager](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin/contract-information/margin-manager.md)
    Description: Learn about the margin manager and how it enables leveraged trading on DeepBook.
- [Margin Pool](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin/contract-information/margin-pool.md)
    Description: Learn about margin pools and how they enable liquidity for leveraged trading.
- [Orders](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin/contract-information/orders.md)
    Description: Learn how to place and manage orders using margin managers.
- [Risk Ratio](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin/contract-information/risk-ratio.md)
    Description: Understanding risk ratios and how they determine leverage limits, collateral
        requirements, and liquidation thresholds in DeepBook Margin.
- [Supply
    Referral](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin/contract-information/supply-referral.md)
    Description: Learn how supply referrals work in DeepBook Margin and how to earn fees by
        referring liquidity suppliers.
- [Take Profit Stop
    Loss](https://docs.haneul.io/standards/deepbook-margin/contract-information/tpsl.md)
    Description: Learn about conditional orders for automated trading strategies on DeepBook Margin.
